title: Etched raises $300M Series C at $10.3B, doubling valuation on inference-chip bet
subtitle: Sequoia leads the round with a16z, Jane Street and SK Hynix; the transformer-only ASIC startup reports $1B in pre-orders and summer 2026 first shipments.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: Etched, the AI chip startup founded in 2022 by three Harvard dropouts, closed a $300 million Series C at a $10.3 billion valuation on July 23. Sequoia Capital led the round with participation from Andreessen Horowitz, Jane Street, SK Hynix and Diffusion. The valuation is roughly double the $5 billion mark from the company's December Series B, and the company said it now has more than $1 billion in confirmed pre-orders for its Sohu ASIC. First rack shipments are scheduled for summer 2026 from an 80,000-square-foot facility opened near Etched's San Jose headquarters.

Etched is one of a growing cluster of startups betting that Nvidia's GPU dominance will not hold on the inference side of AI workloads. Sohu is a transformer-only ASIC — it drops most of the flexibility Nvidia hardware offers and hard-wires the transformer architecture into silicon in exchange for much higher throughput per watt. That is a live wager on whether transformers stay the dominant architecture for the next three to five years. If they do, Etched wins on unit economics. If any of the alternative architectures currently in research — state-space models, hybrid attention variants — breaks out at frontier scale, Etched has built the wrong chip.

The round lands the same week Alphabet raised 2026 capex to $205 billion and OpenAI pushed its compute forecast to $750 billion through 2030. The dollars flowing into inference-specialized silicon reflect a specific market view: the compute cost of running models is going to matter more, not less, as usage compounds. Groq, Cerebras and SambaNova are pursuing variants of the same thesis, each betting on a slightly different architecture. What Etched has that most of them do not is $1 billion in stated pre-orders — customer commitment, not just investor commitment.

For learners: watch which infrastructure bets pair customer commitment with venture capital, and which are venture-only. Pre-orders from real buyers signal something an investor round cannot — that someone in a purchasing role has done technical due diligence and priced the risk of a startup delivering on the promised silicon. If you are evaluating any hardware startup as a career move, ask what fraction of the announced backlog is convertible into cash and what fraction is optioned. That ratio tells you whether the business is real or whether the runway is entirely venture money.

sources.title: AI chip startup Etched defies skeptics, hits $10.3B valuation from big-name investors — TechCrunch
